metrics.title: Enterprise Backlog
metrics.label: +30% vs YE25; record on data-centre demand

metrics.title: Q1 Organic Bookings
metrics.label: Driven by Americas Commercial HVAC / data centres

moat.description: Global HVAC and climate-solutions leader with a dominant Americas Commercial position, a high-margin recurring service base, and an accelerating presence in modular data-centre cooling — moat sources are channel embedment, refrigerant compliance scale, and service-fleet density.
moat.analysisTitle: The Refrigerant Transition and Service-Fleet Moat
moat.analysisSummary: Trane's moat is **specification embedment plus a service-and-controls flywheel that compounds with each refrigerant transition** — and the AI data-centre cooling cycle now layers a TAM expansion on top:

analysisPoints.title: Refrigerant Transition Compliance Scale
analysisPoints.text: Each EPA/global refrigerant phase-down (R-410A → A2L blends, and the next step beyond) forces a re-engineering of the entire chiller, rooftop, and VRF lineup. Trane has the scale to certify across geographies fastest, capture the share-shift window, and price-in the regulatory premium — small competitors lose share each cycle.

analysisPoints.title: Modular Data-Centre Cooling via Stellar Energy
analysisPoints.text: The Stellar Energy acquisition (closed early 2026) makes Trane a top-tier provider of modular chiller plants and packaged cooling skids for hyperscaler campuses. Stellar is guided to ~$500M revenue in 2026 scaling to $1B+ in 2-3 years at mid-teens-plus EBITDA, with reference designs for liquid-to-air chilled-water rejection.

analysisPoints.title: Service Channel and Building Controls Density
analysisPoints.text: Trane's service technician fleet, BAS (building automation system) installed base, and Tracer SC controls platform create high-margin recurring revenue and a switching cost — once a campus is on Trane controls and service, the next chiller replacement defaults to Trane equipment.

growth.description: Q1 2026 organic bookings +24%, backlog $10.7B (+30% vs YE25), FY26 EPS guide raised to $14.75-$14.95 (+13% YoY mid). Americas Commercial HVAC + data-centre cooling drive the growth; Residential and EMEA are slower-growing ballast. Stellar Energy adds a $1B data-centre cooling growth lane on top of the core franchise.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 70 (8-15% CAGR mid-band) + 5 backlog visibility (+30% YoY, record) + 4 data-centre cooling TAM expansion via Stellar - 5 cyclicality risk (residential + EMEA exposure) = 74

drivers.name: Americas Commercial HVAC
drivers.metric: Data-centre + reshoring drive 20%+ bookings growth; backlog at record

drivers.name: Stellar / Data-Centre Cooling
drivers.metric: $500M FY26 → $1B+ in 2-3 years at mid-teens+ EBITDA margin

drivers.name: Residential & EMEA
drivers.metric: Mid-single-digit growth on refrigerant transition tailwind, more cyclical

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If hyperscaler 2027 capex moderates and data-centre cooling bookings flatten, the recently expanded multiple compresses fast — Trane is structurally cyclical (residential + EMEA) and the data-centre cooling growth is now priced in.

valuation.description: At ~$493 TT trades at ~33× FY26 EPS midpoint — premium to industrial peers and a meaningful re-rating versus the historical 25-28× range. Backlog and Stellar story support the multiple but the asymmetry now leans modestly downside.

rows.label: Forward P/E (FY27)
rows.note: Assumes ~13% EPS growth on backlog conversion

peAnalysis.summary: Premium but supported by record backlog and the Stellar data-centre lane; not cheap, with downside re-rating risk on capex moderation.

bear.description: Data-centre cooling bookings normalise in 2027, residential cycle stays soft, multiple compresses to ~22-24× on growth normalisation.
points: Hyperscaler 2027 capex grows <10% YoY, slowing data-centre cooling orders
Residential refrigerant transition pull-forward unwinds in 2027
Stellar integration runs slower than guided, dampening data-centre revenue mix

base.description: FY26 EPS lands near $14.85, FY27 grows to $16.75 on backlog conversion plus Stellar ramp, multiple sustains 31-33×.
points: Backlog continues building through 2026 with bookings >1.1× book-to-bill
Adj operating margin expands ~50 bps on services + data-centre mix
Stellar revenue tracks $700-800M in 2027 at mid-teens-plus EBITDA

bull.description: Data-centre cooling super-cycle extends through 2028, Stellar exceeds $1B run-rate by 2027, FY28 EPS exceeds $20, multiple expands to ~36×.
points: AI data-centre cooling capex sustains 25%+ growth through 2028
Trane wins reference-design slots in next-gen liquid-to-chilled-water rejection systems
Refrigerant phase-down in EMEA accelerates the international upgrade cycle

learnedInterfaces.note: Not applicable — industrial HVAC equipment vendor.

businessLogic.note: Tracer SC and BAS controls software is a real recurring franchise but secondary to the equipment + service moat.

talentScarcity.note: HVAC service technicians and refrigeration engineers are scarce; Trane's training programs and dealer network create a real labour moat.

bundling.note: Equipment + controls + service + (now) modular data-centre cooling skids ship as one engineered solution; competitors cover subsets.

proprietaryData.note: Building telemetry from connected chillers and BAS feeds predictive service, but data leverage is internal and not yet externally monetised.

regulatoryLockIn.note: EPA refrigerant phase-downs (A2L now, the next step ahead) require multi-year recertification of every SKU — Trane's scale lets it certify fastest and capture the share-shift window each transition.

networkEffects.note: Not applicable — equipment + service vendor with no network effects.

transactionEmbedding.note: Once a building or campus is on Trane controls and service contracts, replacement chillers default to Trane; service contracts run for the asset life (15-25 years).

systemOfRecord.note: Not applicable — equipment vendor, not a system of record.

tenMoats.verdict: Trane is a high-quality cyclical compounder with real moats in refrigerant compliance scale and service-fleet embedment, now layered with a data-centre cooling growth lane via Stellar. Valuation has re-rated to a premium that prices much of the data-centre story in; the franchise is durable but the entry multiple matters.
